title: angelica sylvestris
intro: Angelica sylvestris, also known as wild angelica, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its tall stems and umbrella-like clusters of small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and carminative properties, making it effective in treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of wild angelica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and relieve respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Wild angelica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies, such as ointments and salves, to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
